meta.name: Preprocess & QC (Xenium)
meta.description: The preprocessing/QC path commonly used for Xenium and other imaging-based panels: QC metrics → drop low-count cells and rare genes → normalize → log1p → PCA → neighbors → UMAP (2D, plus a 3-component UMAP in obsm['X_umap_3d']). Leaves the data ready to cluster (recipe 2). No HVG selection (targeted panels have too few genes).
meta.provenance: Adapted from the squidpy/scanpy Xenium analysis tutorial (10x Xenium); targets unprocessed raw-count imaging-based data.

readme: Step 1 of the Xenium region-analysis workflow. Computes QC metrics, filters low-count cells (`min_counts`) and rare genes (`min_cells`), then normalizes to median total counts, log1p-transforms, and runs PCA → neighbors → UMAP, plus a 3-component UMAP embedding in `obsm['X_umap_3d']` for the 3D scatter view. No highly-variable-gene step: targeted panels (a few hundred genes) are kept whole. Follow with 'Cluster (Leiden) & top marker genes'. `percent_top` uses small cutoffs suited to short gene panels.

params.name: min_counts
params.tooltip: drop cells with fewer than this many total transcripts

params.name: min_cells
params.tooltip: drop genes detected in fewer than this many cells
